content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

August 21, 1990

Michael O. Cain  
26488 Soboba Street  
Hemet, CA 92344

RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-504

Dear Mr. Cain:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ This letter will confirm the telephone advice I provided to you on August 6, 1990.

FACTS

As a member of the Riverside County Republican Party Central Committee, you filed a candidate and officeholder campaign statement—short form (Form 470) upon your declaration of candidacy for 1990.

QUESTION

Are you required to file an additional campaign statement for the calendar year 1990?

ANSWER

If the Form 470 is filed by a candidate or officeholder with a declaration of candidacy, or on or before the filing deadline for the first campaign statement required for the current calendar year, no additional campaign statements need be filed for that calendar year so long as total receipts and total expenditures remain less than $1,000.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Should you leave office, you must file a statement of termination (Form 415) to terminate your campaign disclosure obligations.

If you have any further questions regarding the above, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[Signature]
By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant
